title: Creamy Charred Australian Custard Pie
description: A rich and creamy dessert featuring a molten custard center, infused with the herbal notes of Australian bush herbs and the distinct char of binchotan charcoal, all while being low in glycemic index.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine the eggs, almond milk, honey, Australian bush herb blend, and binchotan charcoal powder, and blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into 6 individual ramekins or small baking dishes.
Place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the ramekins from the water bath and let cool to room temperature.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled.
Just before serving, top each ramekin with a dollop of coconut cream and a sprinkle of salt.
Use a kitchen torch to lightly char the top of each ramekin, if desired.
